description: While looking at the continuing Western Gull on Pond 115, I saw a curlew-like shorebird in a marshy/mudflat area across the road from the large pond. I glassed the bird and clearly saw a long black decurved bill, boldly striped head with a dark eyeline, and an overall brown color (not the warm buffy coloration of LBCU). Another car passed and spooked the bird, which then flew around Pond 115 several times allowing for good looks in flight, with distinctly barred brown auxilliaries.
